section_header: climate action strategies | how to read the 2022 cap

parent.policy_statement: Pillar 1: Increase access to utility savings and renewable energy, prioritizing households.
parent.verbatim_text:
The initiatives in this plan are organized into three levels: pillars, strategies, and actions. The five pillars support the high-level goals of the plan:

1 Increase access to utility savings and renewable energy, prioritizing households
parent.section_header: CLIMATE ACTION STRATEGIES | HOW TO READ THE 2022 CAP
parent.extraction_rationale: Named pillar that serves as an umbrella for multiple strategies and actions listed in the plan.

subs.policy_statement: Strategy 1.1 Retrofit buildings to reduce building energy use and emissions (Pillar 1).
subs.verbatim_text: 1 Increase access to utility savings and renewable energy, prioritizing households | 1. Retrofit buildings | Action A. Retrofit residential buildings with 4 or fewer units:20% by 2030 and 50%by2040, prioritizing low- or moder- ate-income households B. Retrofit 20%of total 5+ unit residential buildings by 2030 C. Retrofit 20%of total industrial buildings by 2030 D. Retrofit90%oftotalCity-ownedandsisteragen - cy-owned buildings by 2035 E. Retrofit 20%of total commercial buildings by 2035
subs.parent_policy_name: Pillar 1: Increase access to utility savings and renewable energy, prioritizing households.
subs.section_header: CLIMATE ACTION STRATEGIES (Pillar 1 table)
subs.extraction_rationale: A named strategy under Pillar 1 with multiple lettered actions beneath it.

subs.policy_statement: Retrofit residential buildings with 4 or fewer units: 20% by 2030 and 50% by 2040, prioritizing low- or moderate-income households.
subs.verbatim_text:
A

Retrofit residential buildings with 4 or fewer units: 20% by 2030 and 50% by 2040, prioritizing low- or moderate-income households
subs.parent_policy_name: Pillar 1: Increase access to utility savings and renewable energy, prioritizing households.
subs.section_header: S T R A T E G Y 1 | RETROFIT BUILDINGS | ACTIONS
subs.extraction_rationale: Quantified retrofit target with deadlines, presented as an action item.

subs.policy_statement: Retrofit 20% of total 5+ unit residential buildings by 2030.
subs.verbatim_text: Retrofit 20% of total 5+ unit residential buildings by 2030
subs.parent_policy_name: Pillar 1: Increase access to utility savings and renewable energy, prioritizing households.
subs.section_header: S T R A T E G Y 1 | RETROFIT BUILDINGS | ACTIONS
subs.extraction_rationale: Quantified retrofit target with deadline.

subs.policy_statement: Retrofit 20% of total industrial buildings by 2030.
subs.verbatim_text: Retrofit 20% of total industrial buildings by 2030
subs.parent_policy_name: Pillar 1: Increase access to utility savings and renewable energy, prioritizing households.
subs.section_header: S T R A T E G Y 1 | RETROFIT BUILDINGS | ACTIONS
subs.extraction_rationale: Quantified retrofit target with deadline.

subs.policy_statement: Retrofit 90% of total city-owned and sister agency-owned buildings by 2035.
subs.verbatim_text: Retrofit 90% of total city-owned and sister agency-owned buildings by 2035
subs.parent_policy_name: Pillar 1: Increase access to utility savings and renewable energy, prioritizing households.
subs.section_header: S T R A T E G Y 1 | RETROFIT BUILDINGS | ACTIONS
subs.extraction_rationale: Quantified retrofit target with deadline for public buildings.

subs.policy_statement: Strategy 1.2 Connect communities to renewable energy (Pillar 1).
subs.verbatim_text: 1 Increase access to utility savings and renewable energy, prioritizing households | 2. Connect communities to renewable energy | A. Install 5 megawatts of co-owned community solar proj- ects by 2025 B. Increase Chicago-based community renewables to 20 megawatts by 2025 C. Increase community renewables subscriptions to achieve 25%subscribed by low-income and/or environ- mental justice low-income residents by 2030
subs.parent_policy_name: Pillar 1: Increase access to utility savings and renewable energy, prioritizing households.
subs.section_header: CLIMATE ACTION STRATEGIES (Pillar 1 table)
subs.extraction_rationale: Named strategy under Pillar 1 with multiple lettered actions.

subs.policy_statement: Install 5 megawatts of co-owned community solar projects by 2025.
subs.verbatim_text:
Install 5 megawatts of co-owned community solar projects by 2025

By 2025, 5 megawatts of co-owned community solar projects will be installed within city limits, prioritizing households with the highest energy burden.
subs.parent_policy_name: Pillar 1: Increase access to utility savings and renewable energy, prioritizing households.
subs.section_header: S T R A T E G Y 2 | CONNECT COMMUNITIES TO RENEWABLE ENERGY | ACTIONS
subs.extraction_rationale: Quantified capacity target with deadline and prioritization criteria.

subs.policy_statement: Increase Chicago-based community renewables to 20 megawatts by 2025.
subs.verbatim_text:
Increase Chicago-based community renewables to 20 megawatts by 2025

By 2025, the City will increase Chicago-based community renewables to 20 megawatts.
subs.parent_policy_name: Pillar 1: Increase access to utility savings and renewable energy, prioritizing households.
subs.section_header: S T R A T E G Y 2 | CONNECT COMMUNITIES TO RENEWABLE ENERGY | ACTIONS
subs.extraction_rationale: Quantified capacity target with deadline.

subs.policy_statement: Increase community renewables subscriptions so that low-income and/or environmental-justice low-income residents account for 25% subscribed by 2030.
subs.verbatim_text:
Increase community renewables subscriptions to achieve 25% subscribed by low-income and/ or environmental justice low-income residents by 2030

By 2030, the City will increase community renewable subscriptions to achieve a 25% subscription rate by low-income and/or environmental justice low-income residents.
subs.parent_policy_name: Pillar 1: Increase access to utility savings and renewable energy, prioritizing households.
subs.section_header: S T R A T E G Y 2 | CONNECT COMMUNITIES TO RENEWABLE ENERGY | ACTIONS
subs.extraction_rationale: Quantified subscription target with deadline and defined beneficiary group.

section_header: climate action strategies | how to read the 2022 cap

parent.policy_statement: Pillar 2: Build circular economies to create jobs and reduce waste.
parent.verbatim_text:
The five pillars support the high-level goals of the plan:

2 Build circular economies to create jobs and reduce waste
parent.section_header: CLIMATE ACTION STRATEGIES | HOW TO READ THE 2022 CAP
parent.extraction_rationale: Named pillar serving as umbrella for circular economy/waste actions.

subs.policy_statement: Strategy 2.1 Reduce waste and landfilling (Pillar 2).
subs.verbatim_text: 2 Build circular economies to create jobs and reduce waste | 1. Reduce waste and landfilling | A. Introduce community-wide organic waste collection and decomposition by 2025 B. Implement equitable waste source prevention strategies by 2030 C. Divert 90%ofcommercial, industrial, and institutional waste by 2030 D. Divert 75%of construction and demolition waste by 2030 E. Enable building design for disassembly and reuse by 2035 F. Divert 90%ofresidential waste by 2040
subs.parent_policy_name: Pillar 2: Build circular economies to create jobs and reduce waste.
subs.section_header: CLIMATE ACTION STRATEGIES (Pillar 2 table)
subs.extraction_rationale: Named strategy under Pillar 2 with multiple lettered actions and targets.

subs.policy_statement: Introduce community-wide organic waste collection and decomposition by 2025.
subs.verbatim_text: Introduce community-wide organic waste collection and decomposition by 2025
subs.parent_policy_name: Pillar 2: Build circular economies to create jobs and reduce waste.
subs.section_header: S T R A T E G Y 1 | REDUCE WASTE AND LANDFILLING | ACTIONS
subs.extraction_rationale: Time-bound commitment to introduce citywide organics collection/decomposition.

subs.policy_statement: Implement equitable waste source prevention strategies by 2030.
subs.verbatim_text: Implement equitable waste source prevention strategies by 2030
subs.parent_policy_name: Pillar 2: Build circular economies to create jobs and reduce waste.
subs.section_header: S T R A T E G Y 1 | REDUCE WASTE AND LANDFILLING | ACTIONS
subs.extraction_rationale: Time-bound action to implement prevention strategies.

subs.policy_statement: Divert 90% of commercial, industrial, and institutional waste by 2030.
subs.verbatim_text: Divert 90% of commercial, industrial, and institutional waste by 2030
subs.parent_policy_name: Pillar 2: Build circular economies to create jobs and reduce waste.
subs.section_header: S T R A T E G Y 1 | REDUCE WASTE AND LANDFILLING | ACTIONS
subs.extraction_rationale: Quantified diversion target with deadline.

subs.policy_statement: Divert 75% of construction and demolition waste by 2030.
subs.verbatim_text:
Divert 75% of construction and demolition waste by 2030

By 2030, the city will divert 75% of construction and demolition waste from landfills and incineration.
subs.parent_policy_name: Pillar 2: Build circular economies to create jobs and reduce waste.
subs.section_header: S T R A T E G Y 1 | REDUCE WASTE AND LANDFILLING | ACTIONS (cont.)
subs.extraction_rationale: Quantified diversion target with deadline and specified waste stream.

subs.policy_statement: Enable building design for disassembly and reuse by 2035.
subs.verbatim_text: Enable building design for disassembly and reuse by 2035
subs.parent_policy_name: Pillar 2: Build circular economies to create jobs and reduce waste.
subs.section_header: S T R A T E G Y 1 | REDUCE WASTE AND LANDFILLING | ACTIONS (cont.)
subs.extraction_rationale: Time-bound enabling action focused on design for disassembly/reuse.

subs.policy_statement: Divert 90% of residential waste by 2040.
subs.verbatim_text: Divert 90% of residential waste by 2040
subs.parent_policy_name: Pillar 2: Build circular economies to create jobs and reduce waste.
subs.section_header: S T R A T E G Y 1 | REDUCE WASTE AND LANDFILLING | ACTIONS (cont.)
subs.extraction_rationale: Quantified diversion target with deadline.

section_header: climate action strategies | how to read the 2022 cap

parent.policy_statement: Pillar 3: Deliver a robust zero-emission mobility network that connects communities and improves air quality.
parent.verbatim_text:
The five pillars support the high-level goals of the plan:

3 Deliver a robust zero-emission mobility network that connects communities and improves air quality
parent.section_header: CLIMATE ACTION STRATEGIES | HOW TO READ THE 2022 CAP
parent.extraction_rationale: Named pillar that groups multiple transportation strategies and actions.

subs.policy_statement: Strategy 3.1 Make walking, biking, or transit viable options for all trips (Pillar 3).
subs.verbatim_text: 1. Makewalking, biking, or transit viable options for all trips | A. Expand high-quality and low-stress on-street bikeways and off-street trails B. Increase Divvy bikes and shared micromobility trips30% by 2030 C. Enable Chicagoans to walk, bike, take transit, or use shared micromobility for 45%ofall trips by 2040
subs.parent_policy_name: Pillar 3: Deliver a robust zero-emission mobility network that connects communities and improves air quality.
subs.section_header: CLIMATE ACTION STRATEGIES (Pillar 3 table)
subs.extraction_rationale: Named strategy with multiple actions and quantitative targets.

subs.policy_statement: Expand high-quality and low-stress on-street bikeways and off-street trails, including adding by end of 2024: 25 miles protected bike lanes, 25 miles neighborhood greenways, and eliminating 15 miles of gaps in the bikeways network.
subs.verbatim_text: To continue improving community mobility and support citywide transportation emissions reductions, by the end of 2024 the City aims to add 25 more miles of protected bike lanes, 25 more miles of neighborhood greenways, and meet the CDOT Strategic Plan goal of eliminating 15 miles of gaps in the citywide bikeways network.
subs.parent_policy_name: Pillar 3: Deliver a robust zero-emission mobility network that connects communities and improves air quality.
subs.section_header: S T R A T E G Y 1 | MAKE WALKING, BIKING, OR TRANSIT VIABLE OPTIONS FOR ALL TRIPS | ACTIONS
subs.extraction_rationale: Specific infrastructure expansion commitment with quantified mileage and deadline.

subs.policy_statement: Increase Divvy bikes and shared micromobility trips 30% by 2030.
subs.verbatim_text: Increase Divvy bikes and shared micromobility trips 30% by 2030
subs.parent_policy_name: Pillar 3: Deliver a robust zero-emission mobility network that connects communities and improves air quality.
subs.section_header: S T R A T E G Y 1 | MAKE WALKING, BIKING, OR TRANSIT VIABLE OPTIONS FOR ALL TRIPS | ACTIONS
subs.extraction_rationale: Quantified ridership target with deadline.

subs.policy_statement: Enable Chicagoans to walk, bike, take transit, or use shared micromobility for 45% of all trips by 2040.
subs.verbatim_text: The City will invest in the lowest carbon and highest efficiency modes of transportation to increase walking, biking, transit, and shared micro-mobility use to 45% of all trips by 2040
subs.parent_policy_name: Pillar 3: Deliver a robust zero-emission mobility network that connects communities and improves air quality.
subs.section_header: S T R A T E G Y 1 | MAKE WALKING, BIKING, OR TRANSIT VIABLE OPTIONS FOR ALL TRIPS | ACTIONS
subs.extraction_rationale: Mode-share target with a deadline; framed as a City investment commitment.

subs.policy_statement: Strategy 3.2 Increase transit performance and encourage equitable transit-oriented development (Pillar 3).
subs.verbatim_text: 2. Increase transit performance and encourage equitable transit-oriented development | A. Update land use policies to encourage sustainable devel- opment, accessibility, and street safety by 2023 B. Expand use of commuter benefits by 2024 C. Require transportation demandmanagementplans for newdevelopment by 2025 D. Update citywide car and bike parking requirements by 2025
subs.parent_policy_name: Pillar 3: Deliver a robust zero-emission mobility network that connects communities and improves air quality.
subs.section_header: CLIMATE ACTION STRATEGIES (Pillar 3 table)
subs.extraction_rationale: Named strategy with multiple policy actions and deadlines.

subs.policy_statement: Update land use policies to encourage sustainable development, accessibility, and street safety by 2023.
subs.verbatim_text: Update land use policies to encourage sustainable development, accessibility, and street safety by 2023
subs.parent_policy_name: Pillar 3: Deliver a robust zero-emission mobility network that connects communities and improves air quality.
subs.section_header: S T R A T E G Y 2 | ACTIONS
subs.extraction_rationale: Time-bound land use policy update action.

subs.policy_statement: Expand use of commuter benefits by 2024.
subs.verbatim_text: Expand use of commuter benefits by 2024
subs.parent_policy_name: Pillar 3: Deliver a robust zero-emission mobility network that connects communities and improves air quality.
subs.section_header: S T R A T E G Y 2 | ACTIONS
subs.extraction_rationale: Time-bound action to expand commuter benefits.

subs.policy_statement: Require transportation demand management plans for new development by 2025.
subs.verbatim_text: Require transportation demand management plans for new development by 2025
subs.parent_policy_name: Pillar 3: Deliver a robust zero-emission mobility network that connects communities and improves air quality.
subs.section_header: S T R A T E G Y 2 | ACTIONS
subs.extraction_rationale: Regulatory requirement commitment with deadline.

subs.policy_statement: Update citywide car and bike parking requirements by 2025.
subs.verbatim_text:
Update citywide car and bike parking requirements by 2025

Chicago will use this opportunity to modernize parking standards to establish a better balance between all transportation modes (including biking and micromobility options) and make the city safer for pedestrians.
subs.parent_policy_name: Pillar 3: Deliver a robust zero-emission mobility network that connects communities and improves air quality.
subs.section_header: S T R A T E G Y 2 | ACTIONS (cont.)
subs.extraction_rationale: Time-bound commitment to modernize parking requirements through code updates.

subs.policy_statement: Increase CTA ridership 20% by 2030 (relative to 2019 pre-pandemic baseline).
subs.verbatim_text:
Increase CTA ridership 20% by 2030

The 20% increase that this action targets is based on pre-pandemic ridership (2019) though.
subs.parent_policy_name: Pillar 3: Deliver a robust zero-emission mobility network that connects communities and improves air quality.
subs.section_header: S T R A T E G Y 2 | ACTIONS (cont.)
subs.extraction_rationale: Quantified ridership target with deadline and baseline clarification.

subs.policy_statement: Strategy 3.3 Enable zero-emission transit and fleets (Pillar 3).
subs.verbatim_text: 3. Enable zero- emission transit and fleets | B. Support equitable electrification of ride-hail and taxi fleets by 2030 C. Enable 100% electrification of delivery fleets by 2035 D. Electrify 100%oftheCity's fleetby2035 E. Achieve zero-emission transit fleets across Chicagoland by 2040
subs.parent_policy_name: Pillar 3: Deliver a robust zero-emission mobility network that connects communities and improves air quality.
subs.section_header: CLIMATE ACTION STRATEGIES (Pillar 3 table)
subs.extraction_rationale: Named strategy with multiple fleet/transit electrification actions and deadlines.

subs.policy_statement: Enable electric freight loading docks at commercial and industrial buildings, addressing new buildings by 2025 and existing buildings by 2030.
subs.verbatim_text: Enable electric freight loading docks at commercial and industrial buildings, addressing new buildings by 2025 and existing buildings by 2030
subs.parent_policy_name: Pillar 3: Deliver a robust zero-emission mobility network that connects communities and improves air quality.
subs.section_header: S T R A T E G Y 3 | ENABLE ZERO-EMISSION TRANSIT AND FLEETS | ACTIONS
subs.extraction_rationale: Specific infrastructure enablement action with two deadlines (new vs existing buildings).

subs.policy_statement: Support equitable electrification of ride-hail and taxi fleets by 2030.
subs.verbatim_text: Support equitable electrification of ride-hail and taxi fleets by 2030
subs.parent_policy_name: Pillar 3: Deliver a robust zero-emission mobility network that connects communities and improves air quality.
subs.section_header: S T R A T E G Y 3 | ENABLE ZERO-EMISSION TRANSIT AND FLEETS | ACTIONS
subs.extraction_rationale: Time-bound fleet electrification commitment.

subs.policy_statement: Enable 100% electrification of delivery fleets by 2035.
subs.verbatim_text:
Enable 100% electrification of delivery fleets by 2035

The City will enable fleet elec -trification to ensure these providers and others to meet their fleet electrification targets.
subs.parent_policy_name: Pillar 3: Deliver a robust zero-emission mobility network that connects communities and improves air quality.
subs.section_header: S T R A T E G Y 3 | ENABLE ZERO-EMISSION TRANSIT AND FLEETS | ACTIONS
subs.extraction_rationale: Time-bound commitment focused on delivery fleet electrification.

subs.policy_statement: Electrify 100% of the City's fleet by 2035.
subs.verbatim_text: Electrify 100% of the City's fleet by 2035
subs.parent_policy_name: Pillar 3: Deliver a robust zero-emission mobility network that connects communities and improves air quality.
subs.section_header: S T R A T E G Y 3 | ENABLE ZERO-EMISSION TRANSIT AND FLEETS | ACTIONS
subs.extraction_rationale: Clear quantified target and deadline for municipal fleet electrification.

subs.policy_statement: Achieve zero-emission transit fleets across Chicagoland by 2040.
subs.verbatim_text: Achieve zero-emission transit fleets across Chicagoland by 2040
subs.parent_policy_name: Pillar 3: Deliver a robust zero-emission mobility network that connects communities and improves air quality.
subs.section_header: S T R A T E G Y 3 | ENABLE ZERO-EMISSION TRANSIT AND FLEETS | ACTIONS
subs.extraction_rationale: Time-bound regional transit fleet decarbonization commitment.

section_header: climate action strategies | how to read the 2022 cap

parent.policy_statement: Pillar 4: Drive equitable development of Chicago's clean-energy future.
parent.verbatim_text:
The five pillars support the high-level goals of the plan:

4 Drive equitable development of Chicago's clean-energy future
parent.section_header: CLIMATE ACTION STRATEGIES | HOW TO READ THE 2022 CAP
parent.extraction_rationale: Named pillar grouping multiple clean energy/electrification/code actions.

subs.policy_statement: Strategy 4.1 Achieve 100% clean renewable energy (Pillar 4).
subs.verbatim_text: 1. 100%clean renewable energy | A. Aggregate 5,000 megawatts of clean renewable energy within a 250-mile radius of Chicago by 2030 B. Install 30 megawatts of clean renewable energy projects on City property by 2030 C. Achieve 100%clean renewable energy community-wide by 2035
subs.parent_policy_name: Pillar 4: Drive equitable development of Chicago's clean-energy future.
subs.section_header: CLIMATE ACTION STRATEGIES (Pillar 4 table)
subs.extraction_rationale: Named strategy with multiple quantified actions and deadlines.

subs.policy_statement: Aggregate 5,000 megawatts of clean renewable energy within a 250-mile radius of Chicago by 2030.
subs.verbatim_text: Aggregate 5,000 megawatts of clean renewable energy within a 250-mile radius of Chicago by 2030
subs.parent_policy_name: Pillar 4: Drive equitable development of Chicago's clean-energy future.
subs.section_header: S T R A T E G Y 1 | 100% CLEAN RENEWABLE ENERGY | ACTIONS
subs.extraction_rationale: Quantified renewable energy procurement/development target with geographic scope and deadline.

subs.policy_statement: Install 30 megawatts of clean renewable energy projects on City property by 2030.
subs.verbatim_text:
Install 30 megawatts of clean renewable energy projects on City property by 2030

As a first step, the City will work with technical consultants and utility partners to assess the feasibility of installing solar arrays on City-owned properties, prioritizing installations in environmental justice and low-income communities.
subs.parent_policy_name: Pillar 4: Drive equitable development of Chicago's clean-energy future.
subs.section_header: S T R A T E G Y 1 | 100% CLEAN RENEWABLE ENERGY | ACTIONS
subs.extraction_rationale: Quantified target and deadline for renewables on City property with prioritization language.

subs.policy_statement: Achieve 100% clean renewable energy community-wide by 2035.
subs.verbatim_text:
Achieve 100% clean renewable energy communitywide by 2035

By 2035, the City will work with utilities to provide 100% clean renewable energy community-wide.
subs.parent_policy_name: Pillar 4: Drive equitable development of Chicago's clean-energy future.
subs.section_header: S T R A T E G Y 1 | 100% CLEAN RENEWABLE ENERGY | ACTIONS
subs.extraction_rationale: Citywide clean energy target with deadline and delivery mechanism (work with utilities).

subs.policy_statement: Strategy 4.2 Enable building and personal vehicle electrification (Pillar 4).
subs.verbatim_text: 2. Enable buildingand personal vehicle electrification | A. Enact policies that support electrified renovations and newconstruction by 2023 B. Electrify 30%oftotal existing residential buildings by 2035 C. Electrify 20%oftotal existing industrial buildings by 2035 D. Electrify 10%of total existing commercial buildings by 2035 E. Electrify 90%oftotal existing City-owned buildings by 2035
subs.parent_policy_name: Pillar 4: Drive equitable development of Chicago's clean-energy future.
subs.section_header: CLIMATE ACTION STRATEGIES (Pillar 4 table)
subs.extraction_rationale: Named strategy with multiple electrification actions and quantified targets.

subs.policy_statement: Enact policies that support electrified renovations and new construction by 2023.
subs.verbatim_text:
Enact policies that support electrified renovations and new construction by 2023

By 2023, the City will enable electrified renovations for all existing building types (residen -tial, commercial, and industrial) and new construction.
subs.parent_policy_name: Pillar 4: Drive equitable development of Chicago's clean-energy future.
subs.section_header: S T R A T E G Y 2 | ENABLE BUILDING AND PERSONAL VEHICLE ELECTRIFICATION | ACTIONS
subs.extraction_rationale: Time-bound policy action; explicitly states City will enable electrified renovations/new construction.

subs.policy_statement: Electrify 30% of total existing residential buildings by 2035.
subs.verbatim_text: Electrify 30% of total existing residential buildings by 2035
subs.parent_policy_name: Pillar 4: Drive equitable development of Chicago's clean-energy future.
subs.section_header: S T R A T E G Y 2 | ENABLE BUILDING AND PERSONAL VEHICLE ELECTRIFICATION | ACTIONS
subs.extraction_rationale: Quantified electrification target with deadline.

subs.policy_statement: Electrify 90% of total existing City-owned buildings by 2035.
subs.verbatim_text: Electrify 90% of total existing City-owned buildings by 2035
subs.parent_policy_name: Pillar 4: Drive equitable development of Chicago's clean-energy future.
subs.section_header: S T R A T E G Y 2 | ENABLE BUILDING AND PERSONAL VEHICLE ELECTRIFICATION | ACTIONS (cont.)
subs.extraction_rationale: Quantified electrification target with deadline for City-owned buildings.

subs.policy_statement: Enable 2,500 new public passenger electric vehicle charging stations by 2035, prioritizing low- and middle-income communities.
subs.verbatim_text: In coordination with private and public electric vehicle charger operators and building owners, the City will enable the installation of 2,500 new commercial-grade, Level 2 public passenger electric vehicle charging stations by 2035, with priority given to low- and middle-income communities.
subs.parent_policy_name: Pillar 4: Drive equitable development of Chicago's clean-energy future.
subs.section_header: S T R A T E G Y 2 | ENABLE BUILDING AND PERSONAL VEHICLE ELECTRIFICATION | ACTIONS (cont.)
subs.extraction_rationale: Quantified infrastructure target with deadline and equity prioritization.

subs.policy_statement: Strategy 4.3 Align building codes and standards with climate best practices (Pillar 4).
subs.verbatim_text: 3. Align building codes and standards with climate best practices | A. Strengthen policies that support installation of green roofs and walls, tree planting, and other vegetative cover by 2023 B. Enable net-zero-carbon construction by 2040
subs.parent_policy_name: Pillar 4: Drive equitable development of Chicago's clean-energy future.
subs.section_header: CLIMATE ACTION STRATEGIES (Pillar 4 table)
subs.extraction_rationale: Named strategy with two time-bound code/standards actions.

subs.policy_statement: Strengthen policies that support installation of green roofs and walls, tree planting, and other vegetative cover by 2023.
subs.verbatim_text:
Strengthen policies that support installation of green roofs and walls, tree planting, and other vegetative cover by 2023

By 2023, the City will enable green-roof and green-wall installations, tree planting, and other vegetative cover on both existing buildings and new construction.
subs.parent_policy_name: Pillar 4: Drive equitable development of Chicago's clean-energy future.
subs.section_header: S T R A T E G Y 3 | ALIGN BUILDING CODES AND STANDARDS WITH CLIMATE BEST PRACTICES | ACTIONS
subs.extraction_rationale: Time-bound policy strengthening/enabling action for vegetative cover and green roofs/walls.

subs.policy_statement: Enable net-zero-carbon construction by 2040.
subs.verbatim_text:
Enable net-zero-carbon construction by 2040

In alignment with Building Decarbonization Working Group report by the Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change, the City will enable net-zero-carbon construction by 2040.
subs.parent_policy_name: Pillar 4: Drive equitable development of Chicago's clean-energy future.
subs.section_header: S T R A T E G Y 3 | ALIGN BUILDING CODES AND STANDARDS WITH CLIMATE BEST PRACTICES | ACTIONS
subs.extraction_rationale: Clear commitment with deadline to enable net-zero-carbon construction.

subs.policy_statement: Strategy 4.4 Decommission fossil power (Pillar 4).
subs.verbatim_text: 4. Decommission fossil power | A. Develop a fossil-fuel plants transition strategy by 2024
subs.parent_policy_name: Pillar 4: Drive equitable development of Chicago's clean-energy future.
subs.section_header: CLIMATE ACTION STRATEGIES (Pillar 4 table)
subs.extraction_rationale: Named strategy with a specific time-bound action.

subs.policy_statement: Develop a fossil-fuel plants transition strategy (peaker plant transition strategy) by 2024.
subs.verbatim_text:
Develop a fossil-fuel plants transition strategy by 2024

By 2024, the City will develop a peaker plant (power plant used only when there is a high demand for electricity) transition strategy with guidance for an equitable, safe, and inclusive process.
subs.parent_policy_name: Pillar 4: Drive equitable development of Chicago's clean-energy future.
subs.section_header: S T R A T E G Y 4 | DECOMMISSION FOSSIL POWER | ACTIONS
subs.extraction_rationale: Time-bound planning/policy commitment to transition away from peaker plants.

subs.policy_statement: Strategy 4.5 Enable interconnection and storage (Pillar 4).
subs.verbatim_text: 5. Enable interconnection | A. Ensure 150 megawatts of energy storage by 2025 B. Encourage 1,000 megawattsofnewenergydemand reduction by 2030, and 3,000 megawatts by 2040
subs.parent_policy_name: Pillar 4: Drive equitable development of Chicago's clean-energy future.
subs.section_header: CLIMATE ACTION STRATEGIES (Pillar 4 table)
subs.extraction_rationale: Named strategy with quantified storage and demand reduction actions.

subs.policy_statement: Ensure 150 megawatts of energy storage by 2025.
subs.verbatim_text:
Ensure 150 megawatts of energy storage by 2025

Investing in energy storage capacity is necessary to enable a full transition to renewable energy.
subs.parent_policy_name: Pillar 4: Drive equitable development of Chicago's clean-energy future.
subs.section_header: S T R A T E G Y 5 | ENABLE INTERCONNECTION AND STORAGE | ACTIONS
subs.extraction_rationale: Quantified storage capacity target with deadline.

subs.policy_statement: Encourage 1,000 megawatts of new energy demand reduction by 2030, and 3,000 megawatts by 2040.
subs.verbatim_text: By achieving 1,000 megawatts of new energy demand reduction capacity by 2030, and increasing to 3,000 megawatts by 2040, Chicagoans will be better protected during extreme weather events
subs.parent_policy_name: Pillar 4: Drive equitable development of Chicago's clean-energy future.
subs.section_header: S T R A T E G Y 5 | ENABLE INTERCONNECTION AND STORAGE | ACTIONS
subs.extraction_rationale: Quantified demand reduction targets with deadlines.

section_header: accountability and implementation

individual.policy_statement: City of Chicago aims to reduce citywide GHG emissions by a minimum of 62% by 2040 (from 2017 inventory baseline).
individual.verbatim_text: Guided by the strategies and actions of this plan, the City of Chicago aims to reduce the city's level of GHG emissions by 62% by 2040, based on 2017 GHG inventory levels.
individual.section_header: ACCOUNTABILITY AND IMPLEMENTATION
individual.extraction_rationale: Explicit quantified citywide emissions reduction target with deadline and baseline.

section_header: chicago ghg emissions reduction target pathway | the role of offsets

individual.policy_statement: Chicago will not count offset credits toward achieving its 62% GHG reduction goal.
individual.verbatim_text: Chicago will not count offset credits toward its 62% GHG reduction goal achievement. Furthermore, the City will reduce its own emissions before taking action beyond its borders.
individual.section_header: CHICAGO GHG EMISSIONS REDUCTION TARGET PATHWAY | The Role of Offsets
individual.extraction_rationale: Clear policy constraint on accounting for offsets toward the stated goal.

section_header: accountability | governance and reporting

individual.policy_statement: The City will establish community-led climate advisory boards and forums for regular community participation in implementing, reporting, and achieving 2022 CAP actions.
individual.verbatim_text: The City will also establish community-led climate advisory boards and forums for regular community participation in implementing, reporting, and achieving the actions related to the 2022 CAP.
individual.section_header: Accountability | Governance and Reporting
individual.extraction_rationale: Specific governance action commitment (establish advisory boards/forums).

section_header: letter from the mayor

individual.policy_statement: The City is investing $188 million in equity-focused climate interventions, including building retrofits, air quality monitoring expansion, and planting over 75,000 trees.
individual.verbatim_text: To kickstart the implementation of my administration's climate strategy, we are investing $188 million in equity-focused interventions, spanning building retrofits, the expansion of the air quality monitoring network, to the planting of over 75,000 trees.
individual.section_header: Letter from the MAYOR
individual.extraction_rationale: Explicit resource allocation with a quantified funding amount and specified intervention areas.

section_header: investing in climate equity

individual.policy_statement: As part of the 2022 budget, the City has committed significant funding to strengthen existing, introduce new, and anticipate future climate initiatives.
individual.verbatim_text: As a part of the 2022 budget, the City has committed significant funding to strengthen existing, introduce new, and anticipate future initiatives.
individual.section_header: Investing in Climate Equity
individual.extraction_rationale: Budget commitment to fund initiatives; while not specifying an amount here, it states a committed funding action.

section_header: tree equity

individual.policy_statement: Chicago has the goal of planting 75,000 trees by 2026.
individual.verbatim_text: Together with city departments and community partners, Chicago has the goal of planting 75,000 trees by 2026.
individual.section_header: TREE EQUITY
individual.extraction_rationale: Quantified nature-based target with a deadline.

section_header: climate financing and delivery capacity

individual.policy_statement: Chicago City Council approved $188 million for climate resiliency and mitigation as part of the Chicago Recovery Plan (October 2021).
individual.verbatim_text: In October 2021, Chicago's City Council approved $188 million for climate resiliency and mitigation, as part of Mayor Lori Lightfoot's $2.5 billion Chicago Recovery Plan.
individual.section_header: CLIMATE FINANCING AND DELIVERY CAPACITY
individual.extraction_rationale: Specific approved funding allocation with amount and purpose.
